# Research: Foreground galaxy & galaxy-cluster distributions along the co-detected sightlines, and the methods used to search for them

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Scope:** both internal codebase and external prior art
**Codebase state researched against:** commit `8ca87f7`, 2026-06-23 (branch `feature/radiometer-flux-cal`)
**Related Documents:**
- `docs/codetection-science-plan.md` (the parent science plan; §B/§D ground this question)
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`
- `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/adr/0001-two-band-leverage-positioning.md`

**Citation note:** inline references use author-year; full arXiv IDs are in
*References* below. IDs marked `[unverified]` there were reported as
read/verified by the prior-art sub-agents but could **not** be re-confirmed by the
local citation-check hook this session (network unreachable, no cache). Confirm
those IDs against ADS/arXiv before any reach a manuscript.

## Question / Scope

For each of the 12 CHIME/DSA co-detected FRB sightlines, what foreground galaxies
and galaxy clusters lie along the line of sight, and what methods does the project
(and the wider literature) use to search for them? In scope: the production
foreground-search machinery, the search→DM/scattering-budget chain it feeds, the
actual per-sightline distributions currently committed, and the prior-art methods
for (a) intervening-galaxy identification, (b) cluster-intersection searches, and
(c) turning a foreground system into a DM/τ contribution. Out of scope: the
scattering-fit pipeline itself, host-galaxy (z ≥ z_FRB) association, and the
flux-calibration work on the current branch.

A defining property of the sample shapes everything below: **all 12 sightlines sit
at declination +70° to +74°** (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`), a sky region where
the standard optical survey footprints (SDSS, and the catalogs built on it) thin
out or vanish.

## Codebase Findings

### The sample
12 sightlines, Dec +70° to +74°, z from 0.043 to 0.51, with **three placeholder
z = 1.0** values (Freya, Mahi, Johndoeii) that flag an unmeasured host redshift
rather than a real one (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`,
`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:73-81`). Where z is a placeholder the cosmic and
host DM terms are withheld rather than fabricated
(`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:504-515`).

### Production foreground search (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py`)
- **Engines / catalogs.** NED cone search (`galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-54`) plus
  Vizier for GLADE+ (`VII/291`), DESI DR8-North (`VII/292`), and SDSS DR12
  (`V/147`) (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:37-41`, `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:57-102`).
  An opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z TAP engine covers only 3/12 targets
  (Whitney/Phineas/Casey) and is **off by default**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:218-221`, `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:45,55`). Enricher
  catalogs (Legacy DR10 tractor, AllWISE, GALEX, 2MASS-XSC) are likewise off by
  default (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:48-56`, `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py`).
- **Foreground criterion.** A row is foreground if `z < z_FRB + 0.01`, with a
  photo-z-error-aware relaxation (`z − 2σ_z < z_limit`)
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:56-74`, `config.py:13`).
- **Impact-parameter cut.** 100 kpc for galaxies, **5 Mpc for clusters**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-11`, `search.py:71-74`). The query cone is sized to
  the larger limit and capped at 2° so low-z cluster cones don't time out Vizier
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:15-17`, `search.py:228-234`).
- **Dedup & photometry backfill.** Cross-catalog duplicates merged by 10″ + scaled
  Δz, keeping the smaller-photo-z-error / higher-rank source
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:87-149`); PS1 g/i magnitudes backfilled for rows
  lacking a catalog stellar mass — the only mass route for DESI-photo-z-only
  galaxies (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:152-201`, `engines.py:109-148`).

### Cluster handling — the main gap
Galaxy clusters are detected **only** via object-classification strings — NED
`Type` values `GClstr`/`GGroup`, plus `ClG`/`Cl`/`cluster` — caught by a regex and
then granted the 5 Mpc impact threshold (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,71-74`,
`engines.py:36-37`). There is **no dedicated cluster-catalog engine** (no WHL,
redMaPPer, SZ, or X-ray catalog) in the production path. WHL cross-matching exists
**only in exploratory notebooks** (`galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` ~46 WHL
references, `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`), not in `search.py`.
Consequence: **zero cluster-classified rows appear in any of the nine committed
`results/*_galaxies.csv`** — the 5 Mpc cluster search returns nothing in practice
for this sample. (Prior art below explains why this is expected at Dec > +70.)

### Other (non-production) search methods present in the tree
The repo carries three earlier/parallel search methods, useful as a record of
"methods tried":
- WISE-PS1-STRM + Legacy-DR8 photo-z **beam search** with an impact cut
  (`galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`).
- Tarrío PS1 photo-z **SOV-tile impact matching** + Gaia cross-match over exactly
  the 12 targets (`galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`).
- A DESI **DR8 sweep+photo-z stack built specifically for the +70°–+75° Dec strip**
  (`galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py`) — a direct adaptation to the sample's
  high-declination band.

### Search → DM/τ budget chain
The found galaxies feed a documented, primary-source-citing physics chain
(`build_unified.py` → `scattering_predict.py` → `sightline_budget.py`):
- **Stellar-mass ladder** GLADE → 2MASS-K → DESI-SED → WISE-W1 → PS1-Taylor →
  assumed 10¹⁰ M⊙ (`galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155`); halo mass via
  abundance matching, then R_vir / r_s / c (`build_unified.py:266-277`).
- **mNFW hot-halo DM** at the projected impact parameter (Prochaska & Zheng 2019;
  Macquart et al. 2020) (`galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-123`).
- **Cool-CGM DM** scaled by a covering fraction and an MgII-equivalent-width proxy
  (Lan & Mo 2018; Nielsen et al. 2013) (`scattering_predict.py:126-150,321-384`).
- **Two-phase intervening τ**: smooth hot halo + clumpy cool CGM carrying a
  fluctuation-parameter boost `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (McCourt et al. 2018;
  Vedantham & Phinney 2019; Ocker et al. 2021) (`scattering_predict.py:153-300`).
- **Intervening-screen geometric leverage** `g_scatt = D_L·D_LS/D_S/(1+z_L)²`
  (cited to Macquart & Koay 2013) — zero for a screen at/behind the source
  (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`).
- **Budget closure** DM_host = DM_obs − DM_MW,ISM(NE2001) − DM_MW,halo(40,
  Yamasaki & Totani 2020) − DM_cosmic(Macquart) − DM_interv
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:124-143,86,376-608`), with a
  core-extrapolation cap that re-evaluates the hot column at b ≥ 0.1 R_vir
  (`sightline_budget.py:71,463-472`).
- **Prior-predictive sensitivity** over three literature-anchored prior families
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py`,
  `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`).

### Actual per-sightline distribution (committed artifacts)
From `results/search_summary.csv` and `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`
(9/12 sightlines have committed `*_galaxies.csv`; matches the science plan's "host
search 9/12"):

| Sightline | z | N foreground gal. | N clusters | DM_interv (capped) | regime | predicted τ_interv (ms) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zach | 0.043 | 2 | 0 | 143 | CGM | 0.006 |
| Whitney | 0.479 | 2 | 0 | 200 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.27 |
| Oran | 0.300 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Isha | 0.251 | 1 | 0 | 23 | CGM | 0.0015 |
| Wilhelm | 0.510 | 2 | 0 | 103 | CGM | 0.008 |
| Phineas | 0.271 | 3 | 0 | 367 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 1.4 |
| Freya | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 5 | CGM | 3.5e-05 |
| Hamilton | 0.302 | 1 | 0 | 58 | CGM | 0.022 |
| Mahi | 1.000 (placeholder) | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Chromatica | 0.074 | 2 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Casey | 0.287 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Johndoeii | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 213 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.23 |

Observations: (1) **No clusters found on any sightline.** (2) Foreground galaxy
counts are low (0–3), consistent with a shallow high-Dec catalog floor. (3) The
biggest intervening-DM cases (Phineas, Whitney, Johndoeii) are flagged
`GALAXY_INTERIOR` — the sightline passes inside 0.1 R_vir, so the raw mNFW column is
core-extrapolated and the capped value is the trustworthy one. (4) **Negative host
DM** on Zach/Whitney/Wilhelm/Phineas — under-dense sightlines that fall below the
Macquart cosmic mean (`sightline_budget.py:356-368`), already diagnosed in the
science plan as expected scatter, not a bug. (5) The code's own verdict is that
**predicted intervening τ is negligible everywhere except Phineas (1.4 ms)** — i.e.
intervening galaxies rarely dominate scattering in this sample.

## Prior Art

External pass run via direct arXiv/ar5iv full-text reads and primary-source web
search (the semantic search backend was rate-limited; the sub-agents report that
every ID below was verified against the primary source except where flagged
`[unverified]` in *References*, which only means the local hook could not
re-confirm offline this session).

### A. Identifying intervening/foreground galaxies along FRB sightlines
- The standard association cut is **impact parameter b⊥ ≲ r200 with a spec-z**;
  FLIMFLAM operationalizes this with deep narrow-field spectroscopy of galaxies near
  the sightline plus wide-field spectroscopy for the larger-scale density field
  (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1).
- **FLIMFLAM is the canonical end-to-end pipeline**: it reconstructs the ~Mpc matter
  density field along each sightline (Bayesian ARGO reconstruction), associates
  halos, and fits a DM model with MW/IGM/halo/host terms, then constrains the
  CGM/IGM baryon partition — f_IGM = 0.59 (+0.11/−0.10), CGM gas fraction f_gas =
  0.55 (+0.26/−0.29) (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024; method precursors Simha et
  al. 2020, 2021; Khrykin et al. 2023).
- **Baseband/interferometric foreground searches** are the DSA-110 mode: the
  first DSA-110 FRB+host catalog (Law, Sharma et al. 2023) and the multi-halo burst
  FRB 20221219A, whose sightline is "significantly overcrowded" (CDF ≈ 0.99 vs
  expected N_halos = 0.18) with two intervening halos identified by optical/IR SED
  fitting (Connor et al. 2024).
- **Statistical host/intervener association**: PATH Bayesian framework (Aggarwal et
  al. 2021), with chance-coincidence sub-samples (Bhandari et al. 2022).
- The **r200 cut is itself a modeling choice**: Prochaska & Zheng 2019 warn the
  virial "edge" is artificial — integrating to 2·r200 adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at b ≳
  0.5 r200. The code's behavior here is the inverse problem (it caps at 0.1 R_vir
  for the inner blow-up) but the outer-truncation sensitivity is unhandled.

### B. Galaxy clusters along sightlines, and high-Dec catalog coverage
- **Cluster catalogs and their declination reach** (the binding constraint for this
  sample): WHL (Wen, Han & Liu 2012; update Wen & Han 2015) and redMaPPer (Rykoff et
  al. 2014) are **SDSS-based and do not cover Dec > +70** (SDSS imaging in the N
  Galactic Cap ends near +60–65°). SZ catalogs ACT DR5 (Hilton et al. 2021) and
  SPT-SZ (Bleem et al. 2015), and X-ray eROSITA eRASS1 (Bulbul et al. 2024) are
  equatorial/southern. **The only catalogs with genuine Dec > +70 coverage are the
  all-sky ones: Planck PSZ2 (SZ; Planck Collab. 2016) and MCXC / MCXC-II (X-ray
  RASS-based; Piffaretti et al. 2011; MCXC-II 2024)** — both shallow and
  mass-limited there, so poor completeness for group-mass systems.
- **FRBs through clusters give meaningful DM but negligible scattering.** DSA-110
  FRBs in Abell 2310/2311 contribute ICM DM of 16–568 pc cm⁻³ but show no
  significant scattering (Connor et al. 2023). FRB 20190520B pierces two foreground
  clusters (b = 0.39 and 0.77 r200) contributing 450–640 pc cm⁻³ total halo DM, and
  the authors explicitly argue these clusters do **not** cause the burst's large τ
  (Lee et al. 2023).
- **DM vs impact parameter is steep**: ~300 pc cm⁻³ at 0.39 r200 falling to ~110 pc
  cm⁻³ at 0.77 r200 (Lee et al. 2023). Meaningful cluster DM requires the sightline
  within ~1–2 r200 (≲ a few Mpc for the most massive clusters); a **5 Mpc impact
  radius mostly returns negligible-DM near-misses** (this last step is an
  extrapolation from the observed profiles, not a directly stated literature
  result).
- Cluster turbulent ICM can in principle enhance scattering (Zhu, Feng et al. 2018),
  but geometric leverage for a cosmologically-distant cluster screen drives
  broadening to ≪ ms — negligible vs FRB τ (Ocker et al. 2022, scattering horizons;
  secondary).

### C. CGM/halo gas → DM and scattering models
- **mNFW gas profile** ρ_b(r) ∝ 1/[y^(1−α)(y0+y)^(2+α)], fiducial y0 = α = 2, f_b,halo
  = 0.75 (Prochaska & Zheng 2019) — **exactly the form the code implements and
  cites** (`scattering_predict.py:27-123`). FLIMFLAM uses the same form.
- **Macquart relation** ⟨DM_cosmic⟩ ∝ ∫ f_IGM(1+z)/E(z) dz (Macquart et al. 2020,
  Nature 581) — the baseline the code uses for cosmic DM
  (`sightline_budget.py:124-143`).
- **Geometric leverage**: τ ≈ 48.03 μs · A_τ F̃ DM_ℓ² G_scatt /[(1+z_ℓ)³ ν⁴], with
  G_scatt = 2 d_sl d_lo /(L d_so) (Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021). This is the
  canonical FRB-halo leverage paper; the code cites Macquart & Koay 2013 for the
  equivalent geometry (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`) and could additionally cite
  Ocker et al. 2021.
- **Cool-CGM cloudlet scattering**: foundational model is Vedantham & Phinney 2019
  on McCourt et al. 2018 "shattered" sub-pc cloudlets; plus a refractive cloudlet
  lensing paper (see citation caveat in *Synthesis*). But **CGM cloud-size limits**
  suggest realistic low-z cool CGM (n_e ≲ 10⁻² cm⁻³) adds little scattering unless
  clouds are ≲ 0.1 pc (cloud-size-limits study), and hot-CGM scattering is ≪ 1 μs at
  1 GHz (Ocker et al. 2025). The code's `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (range 3–30) is
  therefore an **optimistic** cool-phase prior (`scattering_predict.py:254-262`).

### D. MgII absorbers as intervening cool-gas tracers
- **No published work ties an individual MgII absorber to a measured FRB DM or τ
  excess on the same sightline** — this is a genuine gap (a claimed link from a
  secondary source produced only hallucinated IDs and was discarded). MgII↔cool-CGM
  baseline relations are quasar-based: W(MgII 2796) declines with impact parameter,
  high covering fraction within ~100–150 kpc of L* galaxies (MAGIICAT — Nielsen et
  al. 2013; Lan & Mo 2018) — the relations the code's `predict_mgii_wr` and
  `cool_covering_fraction` encode (`scattering_predict.py:321-384`). MgII in
  groups/clusters: Anand et al. 2022. The DSA multi-halo burst uses COS-Halos
  N_HII(b,M⋆) — the same cool photoionized phase MgII traces — as its DM_igh
  template (Connor et al. 2024).

### E. Disconfirming evidence & limitations
- **Scattering is usually host/Milky-Way-dominated, not foreground-dominated.**
  Halos contribute to DM "but evidently not to scattering, which is dominated by
  host galaxies" (Cordes et al. 2022); measured intervening-halo τ is negligible for
  FRB 181112/191108 (Ocker et al. 2021); FRB 20190520B is explicitly host-dominated
  (Ocker et al. 2022, host-dominated); CHIME population modeling concurs (CHIME
  population study). **Counterpoint:** the DSA multi-halo burst FRB 20221219A is
  *not* host-dominated — an overcrowded intervening sightline can dominate (Connor et
  al. 2024). That is precisely the regime the co-detection sample is built to probe.
- **High-Dec photo-z incompleteness / catalog depth.** DESI Legacy photo-z degrade
  where spec-z training is sparse — directly the Dec > +70 case (Duncan 2022; Zhou
  et al. 2021; SOM photo-z analyses). A Rubin-era forecast finds the dominant error
  is **missing dim/high-z hosts** (catalog depth), degrading inferences ~47–62%, far
  more than photo-z scatter (~7%) (Rubin-era FRB photo-z forecast). SDSS
  spectroscopy largely excludes Dec > +70; GLADE+ is shallow; PS1 lacks
  spectroscopy — so the project's high-Dec search is photometry-limited and
  spec-z-starved. No paper quantifies FRB-foreground completeness specifically at
  Dec > +70 (gap).
- **False-association risk.** PATH quantifies chance coincidence (Aggarwal et al.
  2021); host association degrades once σ_loc ≳ 2″ (localization-precision bias
  study), so large-impact foreground association under coarse localization or
  photo-z-only redshifts is fragile. Radio selection (high-DM/low-scattering) skews
  the sample and biases foreground-incidence statistics (Seebeck, Shannon et al.
  2021). No FRB-specific paper gives a contamination number for intervening (vs
  host) association under photo-z only (gap).

## Synthesis

**The code implements a defensible, literature-grounded version of the standard
method, but a deliberately simplified one.** Its impact-parameter + (photo-z or
spec-z) cone search, mNFW halo DM, Macquart cosmic baseline, two-phase cool/hot
screen, and geometric leverage all map directly onto the canonical references
(Prochaska & Zheng 2019, Macquart et al. 2020, Ocker et al. 2021, FLIMFLAM), and
the modules cite them inline. What it does **not** do — and what FLIMFLAM does — is
reconstruct the Mpc-scale density field or jointly fit the baryon partition; the
code instead attributes per-halo columns deterministically and brackets the
nuisance astrophysics with a prior-predictive sensitivity layer. That is an
appropriate scope choice for a 12-sightline study, not a defect.

**The external consensus validates the code's central scientific verdict.** Across
Cordes et al. 2022, Ocker et al. 2021/2022, and CHIME population work, intervening
halos add DM but rarely scattering — exactly what the budget table reports
(predicted τ_interv negligible everywhere except Phineas). The one regime where this
flips — overcrowded sightlines (Connor et al. 2024) — is the regime this sample
targets, so the scientific payoff lives in the tail, and Phineas (3 foreground
galaxies, GALAXY_INTERIOR, τ_interv = 1.4 ms) is the natural first case to
scrutinize.

**The cluster search is the clearest weakness, and prior art explains why.**
Clusters are found only by NED object-type strings with a 5 Mpc impact, and **zero
clusters turn up** on any sightline. Two independent reasons: (1) at Dec +70°–74°,
the SDSS-based cluster catalogs (WHL, redMaPPer) have no coverage, and even the
notebook WHL cross-match cannot help there — only all-sky Planck PSZ2 and
MCXC/MCXC-II reach the band, both shallow; (2) a 5 Mpc impact mostly yields
negligible-DM near-misses, because meaningful cluster DM needs the sightline within
~1–2 r200, and clusters contribute essentially no scattering at any impact (Connor
et al. 2023, Lee et al. 2023). So the current cluster machinery is both
under-powered (wrong catalogs for this sky) and aimed at a low-yield regime.

### Gaps and open questions
- **Cluster catalog coverage at Dec > +70.** If clusters matter, the production
  search needs an all-sky catalog engine (Planck PSZ2 and/or MCXC-II), not NED-type
  strings — and the 5 Mpc radius should be re-examined against an r200-relative
  criterion. *(Light recommendation; design deferred to planning.)*
- **`COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` is optimistic.** Recent cloud-size/turbulence limits
  (Ocker et al. 2025; cloud-size-limits study) push the realistic cool-CGM
  scattering contribution down; the lo bound (3) may be closer to reality. Worth a
  sensitivity knob already partly present in `sightline_sensitivity.py`.
- **MgII → FRB DM/τ is an unfilled gap in the literature.** The code already
  predicts MgII W_r and uses it to boost cool DM, but that proxy has **no direct
  FRB observational validation** anywhere — it is a modeling assumption, and a
  potential novelty angle if the sample can test it. Flag clearly in any write-up.
- **Outer-truncation (2·r200) DM sensitivity** is unhandled; Prochaska & Zheng warn
  it adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at moderate impact.
- **Citation to verify before publication.** `docs/codetection-science-plan.md:64`
  cites "Sammons 2309.07256" for CGM cloudlet lensing; the prior-art pass reports
  arXiv:2309.07256 [unverified] is the cloudlet refractive-lensing paper but that
  its lead author is **not** Sammons (a separate Sammons et al. paper may exist).
  Re-check the author/ID pairing via an ADS author search before this citation
  reaches a manuscript. *(Left in place pending confirmation; not silently edited
  because the correct attribution was not established this pass.)*

## References / Sources

### Code (commit `8ca87f7`)
- `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-17,21-56` — targets, catalogs, impact/redshift defaults
- `galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,56-74,87-201,204-332` — cluster regex, foreground
  mask, dedup, PS1 backfill, search driver
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-148` — NED / Vizier / PS1 engines
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines_extra.py` — opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z engine
- `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py` — opt-in cross-survey enrichment
- `galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155,246-474` — mass ladder, per-galaxy
  CGM record assembly
- `galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-385` — mNFW DM, cool DM, MgII W_r,
  covering fraction, two-phase τ, g_scatt
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:71-143,340-608` — DM/τ budget closure, caps,
  verdicts
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py` — prior-predictive sensitivity layer
- `galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`,
  `galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`,
  `galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py` — non-production / earlier search methods
- `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`, `galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` —
  exploratory WHL cluster cross-match (notebook-only)
- `results/search_summary.csv`, `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`,
  `results/*_galaxies.csv` — committed per-sightline outputs

### External (arXiv IDs; `[unverified]` = sub-agent-reported but not re-confirmed by the local hook offline this session)
- Prochaska & Zheng 2019, mNFW halo gas/DM — arXiv:1901.11051
- Macquart et al. 2020, Nature 581, Macquart relation — arXiv:2005.13161
- Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1 — arXiv:2402.00505
- Khrykin et al. 2023, FLIMFLAM excess-DM — arXiv:2303.07387
- Simha et al. 2020 (FRB 190608) — arXiv:2005.13157; Simha et al. 2021 (FRB 180924) — arXiv:2108.09881
- Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021, halo scattering + G_scatt — arXiv:2101.04784 [unverified]
- Cordes et al. 2022, DM/τ, host-dominated scattering — arXiv:2108.01172 [unverified]; scattering formalism Cordes et al. 2016 — arXiv:1605.05890 [unverified]
- Vedantham & Phinney 2019, CGM cool-clump scattering — arXiv:1811.10876 [unverified]
- Cloudlet refractive lensing — arXiv:2309.07256 [unverified] (author/ID pairing to re-verify; see Synthesis)
- Ocker et al. 2025, CGM turbulence microphysics — arXiv:2503.02329 [unverified]
- CGM cloud-size limits — arXiv:2504.19562 [unverified]
- Ocker et al. 2022, radio scattering horizons — arXiv:2203.16716
- Connor et al. 2023, DSA-110 FRBs in clusters (ICM DM + RM) — arXiv:2302.14788

- Cluster catalogs: WHL Wen/Han/Liu 2012 — arXiv:1202.6424 (update 2015 — arXiv:1503.04332); redMaPPer Rykoff et al. 2014 — arXiv:1303.3562; ACT DR5 Hilton et al. 2021 — arXiv:2009.11043; SPT-SZ Bleem et al. 2015 — arXiv:1409.0850; Planck PSZ2 2016 — arXiv:1502.01598; eROSITA eRASS1 cosmology companion — arXiv:2402.08458; MCXC Piffaretti et al. 2011 — arXiv:1007.1916, MCXC-II — arXiv:2402.01538

toolUseResult.content:
# Research: Foreground galaxy & galaxy-cluster distributions along the co-detected sightlines, and the methods used to search for them

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Scope:** both internal codebase and external prior art
**Codebase state researched against:** commit `8ca87f7`, 2026-06-23 (branch `feature/radiometer-flux-cal`)
**Related Documents:**
- `docs/codetection-science-plan.md` (the parent science plan; §B/§D ground this question)
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`
- `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/adr/0001-two-band-leverage-positioning.md`

**Citation note:** inline references use author-year; full arXiv IDs are in
*References* below. IDs marked `[unverified]` there were reported as
read/verified by the prior-art sub-agents but could **not** be re-confirmed by the
local citation-check hook this session (network unreachable, no cache). Confirm
those IDs against ADS/arXiv before any reach a manuscript.

## Question / Scope

For each of the 12 CHIME/DSA co-detected FRB sightlines, what foreground galaxies
and galaxy clusters lie along the line of sight, and what methods does the project
(and the wider literature) use to search for them? In scope: the production
foreground-search machinery, the search→DM/scattering-budget chain it feeds, the
actual per-sightline distributions currently committed, and the prior-art methods
for (a) intervening-galaxy identification, (b) cluster-intersection searches, and
(c) turning a foreground system into a DM/τ contribution. Out of scope: the
scattering-fit pipeline itself, host-galaxy (z ≥ z_FRB) association, and the
flux-calibration work on the current branch.

A defining property of the sample shapes everything below: **all 12 sightlines sit
at declination +70° to +74°** (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`), a sky region where
the standard optical survey footprints (SDSS, and the catalogs built on it) thin
out or vanish.

## Codebase Findings

### The sample
12 sightlines, Dec +70° to +74°, z from 0.043 to 0.51, with **three placeholder
z = 1.0** values (Freya, Mahi, Johndoeii) that flag an unmeasured host redshift
rather than a real one (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`,
`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:73-81`). Where z is a placeholder the cosmic and
host DM terms are withheld rather than fabricated
(`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:504-515`).

### Production foreground search (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py`)
- **Engines / catalogs.** NED cone search (`galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-54`) plus
  Vizier for GLADE+ (`VII/291`), DESI DR8-North (`VII/292`), and SDSS DR12
  (`V/147`) (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:37-41`, `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:57-102`).
  An opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z TAP engine covers only 3/12 targets
  (Whitney/Phineas/Casey) and is **off by default**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:218-221`, `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:45,55`). Enricher
  catalogs (Legacy DR10 tractor, AllWISE, GALEX, 2MASS-XSC) are likewise off by
  default (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:48-56`, `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py`).
- **Foreground criterion.** A row is foreground if `z < z_FRB + 0.01`, with a
  photo-z-error-aware relaxation (`z − 2σ_z < z_limit`)
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:56-74`, `config.py:13`).
- **Impact-parameter cut.** 100 kpc for galaxies, **5 Mpc for clusters**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-11`, `search.py:71-74`). The query cone is sized to
  the larger limit and capped at 2° so low-z cluster cones don't time out Vizier
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:15-17`, `search.py:228-234`).
- **Dedup & photometry backfill.** Cross-catalog duplicates merged by 10″ + scaled
  Δz, keeping the smaller-photo-z-error / higher-rank source
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:87-149`); PS1 g/i magnitudes backfilled for rows
  lacking a catalog stellar mass — the only mass route for DESI-photo-z-only
  galaxies (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:152-201`, `engines.py:109-148`).

### Cluster handling — the main gap
Galaxy clusters are detected **only** via object-classification strings — NED
`Type` values `GClstr`/`GGroup`, plus `ClG`/`Cl`/`cluster` — caught by a regex and
then granted the 5 Mpc impact threshold (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,71-74`,
`engines.py:36-37`). There is **no dedicated cluster-catalog engine** (no WHL,
redMaPPer, SZ, or X-ray catalog) in the production path. WHL cross-matching exists
**only in exploratory notebooks** (`galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` ~46 WHL
references, `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`), not in `search.py`.
Consequence: **zero cluster-classified rows appear in any of the nine committed
`results/*_galaxies.csv`** — the 5 Mpc cluster search returns nothing in practice
for this sample. (Prior art below explains why this is expected at Dec > +70.)

### Other (non-production) search methods present in the tree
The repo carries three earlier/parallel search methods, useful as a record of
"methods tried":
- WISE-PS1-STRM + Legacy-DR8 photo-z **beam search** with an impact cut
  (`galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`).
- Tarrío PS1 photo-z **SOV-tile impact matching** + Gaia cross-match over exactly
  the 12 targets (`galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`).
- A DESI **DR8 sweep+photo-z stack built specifically for the +70°–+75° Dec strip**
  (`galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py`) — a direct adaptation to the sample's
  high-declination band.

### Search → DM/τ budget chain
The found galaxies feed a documented, primary-source-citing physics chain
(`build_unified.py` → `scattering_predict.py` → `sightline_budget.py`):
- **Stellar-mass ladder** GLADE → 2MASS-K → DESI-SED → WISE-W1 → PS1-Taylor →
  assumed 10¹⁰ M⊙ (`galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155`); halo mass via
  abundance matching, then R_vir / r_s / c (`build_unified.py:266-277`).
- **mNFW hot-halo DM** at the projected impact parameter (Prochaska & Zheng 2019;
  Macquart et al. 2020) (`galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-123`).
- **Cool-CGM DM** scaled by a covering fraction and an MgII-equivalent-width proxy
  (Lan & Mo 2018; Nielsen et al. 2013) (`scattering_predict.py:126-150,321-384`).
- **Two-phase intervening τ**: smooth hot halo + clumpy cool CGM carrying a
  fluctuation-parameter boost `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (McCourt et al. 2018;
  Vedantham & Phinney 2019; Ocker et al. 2021) (`scattering_predict.py:153-300`).
- **Intervening-screen geometric leverage** `g_scatt = D_L·D_LS/D_S/(1+z_L)²`
  (cited to Macquart & Koay 2013) — zero for a screen at/behind the source
  (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`).
- **Budget closure** DM_host = DM_obs − DM_MW,ISM(NE2001) − DM_MW,halo(40,
  Yamasaki & Totani 2020) − DM_cosmic(Macquart) − DM_interv
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:124-143,86,376-608`), with a
  core-extrapolation cap that re-evaluates the hot column at b ≥ 0.1 R_vir
  (`sightline_budget.py:71,463-472`).
- **Prior-predictive sensitivity** over three literature-anchored prior families
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py`,
  `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`).

### Actual per-sightline distribution (committed artifacts)
From `results/search_summary.csv` and `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`
(9/12 sightlines have committed `*_galaxies.csv`; matches the science plan's "host
search 9/12"):

| Sightline | z | N foreground gal. | N clusters | DM_interv (capped) | regime | predicted τ_interv (ms) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zach | 0.043 | 2 | 0 | 143 | CGM | 0.006 |
| Whitney | 0.479 | 2 | 0 | 200 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.27 |
| Oran | 0.300 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Isha | 0.251 | 1 | 0 | 23 | CGM | 0.0015 |
| Wilhelm | 0.510 | 2 | 0 | 103 | CGM | 0.008 |
| Phineas | 0.271 | 3 | 0 | 367 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 1.4 |
| Freya | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 5 | CGM | 3.5e-05 |
| Hamilton | 0.302 | 1 | 0 | 58 | CGM | 0.022 |
| Mahi | 1.000 (placeholder) | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Chromatica | 0.074 | 2 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Casey | 0.287 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Johndoeii | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 213 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.23 |

Observations: (1) **No clusters found on any sightline.** (2) Foreground galaxy
counts are low (0–3), consistent with a shallow high-Dec catalog floor. (3) The
biggest intervening-DM cases (Phineas, Whitney, Johndoeii) are flagged
`GALAXY_INTERIOR` — the sightline passes inside 0.1 R_vir, so the raw mNFW column is
core-extrapolated and the capped value is the trustworthy one. (4) **Negative host
DM** on Zach/Whitney/Wilhelm/Phineas — under-dense sightlines that fall below the
Macquart cosmic mean (`sightline_budget.py:356-368`), already diagnosed in the
science plan as expected scatter, not a bug. (5) The code's own verdict is that
**predicted intervening τ is negligible everywhere except Phineas (1.4 ms)** — i.e.
intervening galaxies rarely dominate scattering in this sample.

## Prior Art

External pass run via direct arXiv/ar5iv full-text reads and primary-source web
search (the semantic search backend was rate-limited; the sub-agents report that
every ID below was verified against the primary source except where flagged
`[unverified]` in *References*, which only means the local hook could not
re-confirm offline this session).

### A. Identifying intervening/foreground galaxies along FRB sightlines
- The standard association cut is **impact parameter b⊥ ≲ r200 with a spec-z**;
  FLIMFLAM operationalizes this with deep narrow-field spectroscopy of galaxies near
  the sightline plus wide-field spectroscopy for the larger-scale density field
  (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1).
- **FLIMFLAM is the canonical end-to-end pipeline**: it reconstructs the ~Mpc matter
  density field along each sightline (Bayesian ARGO reconstruction), associates
  halos, and fits a DM model with MW/IGM/halo/host terms, then constrains the
  CGM/IGM baryon partition — f_IGM = 0.59 (+0.11/−0.10), CGM gas fraction f_gas =
  0.55 (+0.26/−0.29) (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024; method precursors Simha et
  al. 2020, 2021; Khrykin et al. 2023).
- **Baseband/interferometric foreground searches** are the DSA-110 mode: the
  first DSA-110 FRB+host catalog (Law, Sharma et al. 2023) and the multi-halo burst
  FRB 20221219A, whose sightline is "significantly overcrowded" (CDF ≈ 0.99 vs
  expected N_halos = 0.18) with two intervening halos identified by optical/IR SED
  fitting (Connor et al. 2024).
- **Statistical host/intervener association**: PATH Bayesian framework (Aggarwal et
  al. 2021), with chance-coincidence sub-samples (Bhandari et al. 2022).
- The **r200 cut is itself a modeling choice**: Prochaska & Zheng 2019 warn the
  virial "edge" is artificial — integrating to 2·r200 adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at b ≳
  0.5 r200. The code's behavior here is the inverse problem (it caps at 0.1 R_vir
  for the inner blow-up) but the outer-truncation sensitivity is unhandled.

### B. Galaxy clusters along sightlines, and high-Dec catalog coverage
- **Cluster catalogs and their declination reach** (the binding constraint for this
  sample): WHL (Wen, Han & Liu 2012; update Wen & Han 2015) and redMaPPer (Rykoff et
  al. 2014) are **SDSS-based and do not cover Dec > +70** (SDSS imaging in the N
  Galactic Cap ends near +60–65°). SZ catalogs ACT DR5 (Hilton et al. 2021) and
  SPT-SZ (Bleem et al. 2015), and X-ray eROSITA eRASS1 (Bulbul et al. 2024) are
  equatorial/southern. **The only catalogs with genuine Dec > +70 coverage are the
  all-sky ones: Planck PSZ2 (SZ; Planck Collab. 2016) and MCXC / MCXC-II (X-ray
  RASS-based; Piffaretti et al. 2011; MCXC-II 2024)** — both shallow and
  mass-limited there, so poor completeness for group-mass systems.
- **FRBs through clusters give meaningful DM but negligible scattering.** DSA-110
  FRBs in Abell 2310/2311 contribute ICM DM of 16–568 pc cm⁻³ but show no
  significant scattering (Connor et al. 2023). FRB 20190520B pierces two foreground
  clusters (b = 0.39 and 0.77 r200) contributing 450–640 pc cm⁻³ total halo DM, and
  the authors explicitly argue these clusters do **not** cause the burst's large τ
  (Lee et al. 2023).
- **DM vs impact parameter is steep**: ~300 pc cm⁻³ at 0.39 r200 falling to ~110 pc
  cm⁻³ at 0.77 r200 (Lee et al. 2023). Meaningful cluster DM requires the sightline
  within ~1–2 r200 (≲ a few Mpc for the most massive clusters); a **5 Mpc impact
  radius mostly returns negligible-DM near-misses** (this last step is an
  extrapolation from the observed profiles, not a directly stated literature
  result).
- Cluster turbulent ICM can in principle enhance scattering (Zhu, Feng et al. 2018),
  but geometric leverage for a cosmologically-distant cluster screen drives
  broadening to ≪ ms — negligible vs FRB τ (Ocker et al. 2022, scattering horizons;
  secondary).

### C. CGM/halo gas → DM and scattering models
- **mNFW gas profile** ρ_b(r) ∝ 1/[y^(1−α)(y0+y)^(2+α)], fiducial y0 = α = 2, f_b,halo
  = 0.75 (Prochaska & Zheng 2019) — **exactly the form the code implements and
  cites** (`scattering_predict.py:27-123`). FLIMFLAM uses the same form.
- **Macquart relation** ⟨DM_cosmic⟩ ∝ ∫ f_IGM(1+z)/E(z) dz (Macquart et al. 2020,
  Nature 581) — the baseline the code uses for cosmic DM
  (`sightline_budget.py:124-143`).
- **Geometric leverage**: τ ≈ 48.03 μs · A_τ F̃ DM_ℓ² G_scatt /[(1+z_ℓ)³ ν⁴], with
  G_scatt = 2 d_sl d_lo /(L d_so) (Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021). This is the
  canonical FRB-halo leverage paper; the code cites Macquart & Koay 2013 for the
  equivalent geometry (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`) and could additionally cite
  Ocker et al. 2021.
- **Cool-CGM cloudlet scattering**: foundational model is Vedantham & Phinney 2019
  on McCourt et al. 2018 "shattered" sub-pc cloudlets; plus a refractive cloudlet
  lensing paper (see citation caveat in *Synthesis*). But **CGM cloud-size limits**
  suggest realistic low-z cool CGM (n_e ≲ 10⁻² cm⁻³) adds little scattering unless
  clouds are ≲ 0.1 pc (cloud-size-limits study), and hot-CGM scattering is ≪ 1 μs at
  1 GHz (Ocker et al. 2025). The code's `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (range 3–30) is
  therefore an **optimistic** cool-phase prior (`scattering_predict.py:254-262`).

### D. MgII absorbers as intervening cool-gas tracers
- **No published work ties an individual MgII absorber to a measured FRB DM or τ
  excess on the same sightline** — this is a genuine gap (a claimed link from a
  secondary source produced only hallucinated IDs and was discarded). MgII↔cool-CGM
  baseline relations are quasar-based: W(MgII 2796) declines with impact parameter,
  high covering fraction within ~100–150 kpc of L* galaxies (MAGIICAT — Nielsen et
  al. 2013; Lan & Mo 2018) — the relations the code's `predict_mgii_wr` and
  `cool_covering_fraction` encode (`scattering_predict.py:321-384`). MgII in
  groups/clusters: Anand et al. 2022. The DSA multi-halo burst uses COS-Halos
  N_HII(b,M⋆) — the same cool photoionized phase MgII traces — as its DM_igh
  template (Connor et al. 2024).

### E. Disconfirming evidence & limitations
- **Scattering is usually host/Milky-Way-dominated, not foreground-dominated.**
  Halos contribute to DM "but evidently not to scattering, which is dominated by
  host galaxies" (Cordes et al. 2022); measured intervening-halo τ is negligible for
  FRB 181112/191108 (Ocker et al. 2021); FRB 20190520B is explicitly host-dominated
  (Ocker et al. 2022, host-dominated); CHIME population modeling concurs (CHIME
  population study). **Counterpoint:** the DSA multi-halo burst FRB 20221219A is
  *not* host-dominated — an overcrowded intervening sightline can dominate (Connor et
  al. 2024). That is precisely the regime the co-detection sample is built to probe.
- **High-Dec photo-z incompleteness / catalog depth.** DESI Legacy photo-z degrade
  where spec-z training is sparse — directly the Dec > +70 case (Duncan 2022; Zhou
  et al. 2021; SOM photo-z analyses). A Rubin-era forecast finds the dominant error
  is **missing dim/high-z hosts** (catalog depth), degrading inferences ~47–62%, far
  more than photo-z scatter (~7%) (Rubin-era FRB photo-z forecast). SDSS
  spectroscopy largely excludes Dec > +70; GLADE+ is shallow; PS1 lacks
  spectroscopy — so the project's high-Dec search is photometry-limited and
  spec-z-starved. No paper quantifies FRB-foreground completeness specifically at
  Dec > +70 (gap).
- **False-association risk.** PATH quantifies chance coincidence (Aggarwal et al.
  2021); host association degrades once σ_loc ≳ 2″ (localization-precision bias
  study), so large-impact foreground association under coarse localization or
  photo-z-only redshifts is fragile. Radio selection (high-DM/low-scattering) skews
  the sample and biases foreground-incidence statistics (Seebeck, Shannon et al.
  2021). No FRB-specific paper gives a contamination number for intervening (vs
  host) association under photo-z only (gap).

## Synthesis

**The code implements a defensible, literature-grounded version of the standard
method, but a deliberately simplified one.** Its impact-parameter + (photo-z or
spec-z) cone search, mNFW halo DM, Macquart cosmic baseline, two-phase cool/hot
screen, and geometric leverage all map directly onto the canonical references
(Prochaska & Zheng 2019, Macquart et al. 2020, Ocker et al. 2021, FLIMFLAM), and
the modules cite them inline. What it does **not** do — and what FLIMFLAM does — is
reconstruct the Mpc-scale density field or jointly fit the baryon partition; the
code instead attributes per-halo columns deterministically and brackets the
nuisance astrophysics with a prior-predictive sensitivity layer. That is an
appropriate scope choice for a 12-sightline study, not a defect.

**The external consensus validates the code's central scientific verdict.** Across
Cordes et al. 2022, Ocker et al. 2021/2022, and CHIME population work, intervening
halos add DM but rarely scattering — exactly what the budget table reports
(predicted τ_interv negligible everywhere except Phineas). The one regime where this
flips — overcrowded sightlines (Connor et al. 2024) — is the regime this sample
targets, so the scientific payoff lives in the tail, and Phineas (3 foreground
galaxies, GALAXY_INTERIOR, τ_interv = 1.4 ms) is the natural first case to
scrutinize.

**The cluster search is the clearest weakness, and prior art explains why.**
Clusters are found only by NED object-type strings with a 5 Mpc impact, and **zero
clusters turn up** on any sightline. Two independent reasons: (1) at Dec +70°–74°,
the SDSS-based cluster catalogs (WHL, redMaPPer) have no coverage, and even the
notebook WHL cross-match cannot help there — only all-sky Planck PSZ2 and
MCXC/MCXC-II reach the band, both shallow; (2) a 5 Mpc impact mostly yields
negligible-DM near-misses, because meaningful cluster DM needs the sightline within
~1–2 r200, and clusters contribute essentially no scattering at any impact (Connor
et al. 2023, Lee et al. 2023). So the current cluster machinery is both
under-powered (wrong catalogs for this sky) and aimed at a low-yield regime.

### Gaps and open questions
- **Cluster catalog coverage at Dec > +70.** If clusters matter, the production
  search needs an all-sky catalog engine (Planck PSZ2 and/or MCXC-II), not NED-type
  strings — and the 5 Mpc radius should be re-examined against an r200-relative
  criterion. *(Light recommendation; design deferred to planning.)*
- **`COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` is optimistic.** Recent cloud-size/turbulence limits
  (Ocker et al. 2025; cloud-size-limits study) push the realistic cool-CGM
  scattering contribution down; the lo bound (3) may be closer to reality. Worth a
  sensitivity knob already partly present in `sightline_sensitivity.py`.
- **MgII → FRB DM/τ is an unfilled gap in the literature.** The code already
  predicts MgII W_r and uses it to boost cool DM, but that proxy has **no direct
  FRB observational validation** anywhere — it is a modeling assumption, and a
  potential novelty angle if the sample can test it. Flag clearly in any write-up.
- **Outer-truncation (2·r200) DM sensitivity** is unhandled; Prochaska & Zheng warn
  it adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at moderate impact.
- **Citation to verify before publication.** `docs/codetection-science-plan.md:64`
  cites "Sammons 2309.07256" for CGM cloudlet lensing; the prior-art pass reports
  arXiv:2309.07256 [unverified] is the cloudlet refractive-lensing paper but that
  its lead author is **not** Sammons (a separate Sammons et al. paper may exist).
  Re-check the author/ID pairing via an ADS author search before this citation
  reaches a manuscript. *(Left in place pending confirmation; not silently edited
  because the correct attribution was not established this pass.)*

## References / Sources

### Code (commit `8ca87f7`)
- `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-17,21-56` — targets, catalogs, impact/redshift defaults
- `galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,56-74,87-201,204-332` — cluster regex, foreground
  mask, dedup, PS1 backfill, search driver
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-148` — NED / Vizier / PS1 engines
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines_extra.py` — opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z engine
- `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py` — opt-in cross-survey enrichment
- `galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155,246-474` — mass ladder, per-galaxy
  CGM record assembly
- `galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-385` — mNFW DM, cool DM, MgII W_r,
  covering fraction, two-phase τ, g_scatt
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:71-143,340-608` — DM/τ budget closure, caps,
  verdicts
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py` — prior-predictive sensitivity layer
- `galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`,
  `galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`,
  `galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py` — non-production / earlier search methods
- `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`, `galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` —
  exploratory WHL cluster cross-match (notebook-only)
- `results/search_summary.csv`, `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`,
  `results/*_galaxies.csv` — committed per-sightline outputs

### External (arXiv IDs; `[unverified]` = sub-agent-reported but not re-confirmed by the local hook offline this session)
- Prochaska & Zheng 2019, mNFW halo gas/DM — arXiv:1901.11051

- Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1 — arXiv:2402.00505
- Khrykin et al. 2023, FLIMFLAM excess-DM — arXiv:2303.07387
- Simha et al. 2020 (FRB 190608) — arXiv:2005.13157; Simha et al. 2021 (FRB 180924) — arXiv:2108.09881
- Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021, halo scattering + G_scatt — arXiv:2101.04784 [unverified]
- Cordes et al. 2022, DM/τ, host-dominated scattering — arXiv:2108.01172 [unverified]; scattering formalism Cordes et al. 2016 — arXiv:1605.05890 [unverified]
- Vedantham & Phinney 2019, CGM cool-clump scattering — arXiv:1811.10876 [unverified]

content.text:
Written and self-reviewed: `docs/rse/specs/research-foreground-galaxies-sightlines.md` (commit SHA + date recorded, every codebase finding carries a `file:line`, every prior-art claim cited, all five external sub-questions + the internal pass answered).

Three headline findings:
- **Code matches the canonical method** (impact-param + photo-z, mNFW halo DM, Macquart baseline, two-phase τ, geometric leverage) — a deliberately simplified FLIMFLAM, with literature cited inline in the modules themselves.
- **Cluster search is the real weakness**: NED-`Type`-only with a 5 Mpc impact, finding **zero clusters** — and prior art explains both reasons (SDSS-based WHL/redMaPPer have no Dec > +70 coverage; 5 Mpc is mostly negligible-DM near-misses; clusters barely scatter anyway). Fix path: an all-sky engine (Planck PSZ2 / MCXC-II) + an r200-relative impact cut.
- **Literature validates the code's verdict** that intervening galaxies add DM but rarely scattering — except overcrowded sightlines (DSA multi-halo, Connor 2024), which is exactly this sample's target regime; **Phineas** is the standout case.

Two caveats baked into the doc:
- The citation-check hook couldn't verify 18 arXiv IDs offline (network down) — flagged `[unverified]` in References; the sub-agents read them but confirm against ADS before any manuscript.
- `docs/codetection-science-plan.md:64` cites "Sammons 2309.07256" for cloudlet lensing — the prior-art pass reports that ID's lead author is **not** Sammons. Left in place (correct attribution not established this session), flagged for an ADS re-check rather than silently edited.

The doc is untracked; I didn't commit (the branch carries unrelated flux-cal lanes — left untouched).

Please review `research-foreground-galaxies-sightlines.md` and tell me if anything's missing or off. When you're happy, the natural next step is **`ai-research-workflows:planning-implementations`** — most likely scoped to the all-sky cluster-catalog engine + r200-relative impact cut, which is the most actionable gap surfaced here.

A clean compile / build / lint pass / "it ran" / your own confidence is NOT verification —
it shows the edit is well-formed, not that it is CORRECT. Before finishing you must run an
INDEPENDENT correctness check on each path and record it. Strongest first:
  - dispatch an independent reviewer subagent to try to BREAK the edit (adversarial-review)
  - a test that ASSERTS the right answer (test)
  - compare against a known-truth / reference / analytic value (oracle)
  - independently re-derive or reconstruct and check it agrees (reproduce / cross-check)
  - the user explicitly confirms (human)

content.text: Verify gate is right — the doc's value is the accuracy of its codebase claims. Dispatching an adversarial reviewer to check the `file:line` anchors and the per-sightline data table against the actual files.

input.prompt:

Every codebase claim in `docs/rse/specs/research-foreground-galaxies-sightlines.md` checks out against the repository at the stated commit. I attempted to break each one and found no discrepancy.

- regime and predicted τ_interv columns match exactly (incl. GALAXY_INTERIOR for Whitney/Phineas/Johndoeii, CGM/none elsewhere).
- z values match `config.py` (non-placeholder range 0.043–0.51).

**Item 2 — no cluster rows:** Searched all 9 committed `results/*_galaxies.csv` for gclstr/ggroup/clg/cluster (case-insensitive): zero matches. The CSVs carry only a lowercase DESI `type` (morphological: EXP/REX/DEV/PSF), which is not in the production code's `CLASSIFICATION_COLUMNS` and matches no cluster string. Claim holds.

**Item 3 — cluster handling:** `search.py:31-43` has the exact `CLASSIFICATION_COLUMNS = ("classification","Type","class","cl","otype")` and `_CLUSTER_RE = re.compile(r"cluster|gclstr|clg|^cl\b", IGNORECASE)`; `engines.py:36-37` renames NED `Type`→`classification`. Grep for WHL/redMaPPer/PSZ/MCXC/eROSITA/ACT/SPT across `search.py`, `engines.py`, `engines_extra.py`, `config.py` returned nothing — no cluster-catalog engine in the production path. Claim holds.

**Item 4 — six spot-checked anchors, all correct:**
- `config.py:9-11` — `DEFAULT_IMPACT_KPC=100`, `DEFAULT_CLUSTER_IMPACT_KPC=5000.0` (5 Mpc) ✓
- `config.py:21-34` — 12 TARGETS, Dec +70d18m to +74d12m ✓
- `search.py:56-74` — `_foreground_mask` with `z_limit=z_frb+z_eps` and `(z − 2σ_z) < z_limit` relaxation, cluster impact override ✓
- `scattering_predict.py:27` — `def dm_halo_mnfw(...)` ✓
- `scattering_predict.py:254-262` — `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST=10.0`, `_LO=3.0`, `_HI=30.0` ✓
- `sightline_budget.py:86` — `DM_MW_HALO = 40.0` ✓

**Item 5 — placeholder z:** `sightline_budget.py:77` `PLACEHOLDER_Z = 1.0`, comment names Freya/Mahi/Johndoeii; `config.py:28,30,33` all set those three to z_frb=1.0000. Claim holds.

**Item 6 — 9/12 committed CSVs:** `git ls-files results/*_galaxies.csv` returns exactly 9 (chromatica, freya, hamilton, isha, johndoeii, phineas, whitney, wilhelm, zach). Claim holds.

Minor note (not a discrepancy): the doc says "~46 WHL references" in `analysis.ipynb`; the actual count is 48 — within the tilde-approximation and not a defect. Per instructions I did not check the external arXiv IDs (deliberately flagged `[unverified]`).

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/jakobfaber/.claude/my-skillset/plugins-local/ai-research-workflows/skills/planning-implementations

# Planning Implementations

Produce a comprehensive, phased implementation plan grounded in actual codebase
research, with measurable success criteria and no unresolved decisions.

## Interaction mode

This skill leans **Collaborative** by default. For the full Collaborative-vs-Direct protocol and override rules, see the Interaction Modes reference in the `ai-research-workflows:using-research-workflows` skill.

## Starting the skill

If no topic is given, enter Collaborative mode and ask what to plan (and check
`docs/rse/specs/` (then legacy `.agents/`) for existing research/prior-art). If the topic is given without a
research doc, look for `docs/rse/specs/research-*.md` matches and suggest running
`ai-research-workflows:researching` first if none are found — then proceed on user
preference.

## Process

### Step 1: Context Gathering

- Read all mentioned files **completely** before anything else.
- Check `docs/rse/specs/research-*.md` (and legacy `.agents/research-*.md`, `.agents/prior-art-*.md`) for relevant context.
- Investigate related files, existing patterns, integration points, and test
  coverage in parallel where possible.
- Only ask questions that code cannot answer (product decisions, ambiguous
  scenarios). Never ask "which database?" — read the code.
- In Collaborative mode, present findings and focused questions before
  proceeding.

### Step 2: Research & Discovery

- Investigate in parallel: existing patterns, integration points, available
  dependencies, current test coverage.
- **Wait for all research to complete** before synthesizing.
- In Collaborative mode, present design options with pros/cons and a
  recommendation; get approval before continuing.

### Step 3: Plan Structure (Collaborative mode)

- Propose a phased breakdown with a one-line objective per phase.
- Get feedback before writing detailed steps.

### Step 4: Plan Writing

Generate filename from the topic slug (`plan-<slug>.md`). Read the template:
`assets/plan-template.md`.

**Required sections:**

1. Overview — what, why, high-level how
2. Current State Analysis — existing code with `file:line` references
3. Desired End State — observable success outcomes
4. What We're NOT Doing — explicit scope boundaries
5. Implementation Approach — technical strategy and key decisions
6. Implementation Phases — phased tasks with file paths and verification steps
7. Success Criteria — split into Automated and Manual (see below)
8. Testing Strategy — unit, integration, manual
9. References — research docs, files analyzed, external docs

Each phase needs: a clear objective, tasks with `path/to/file.ext:lines`,
dependencies on prior phases, and verification steps.

#### Bite-sized, test-first tasks

Each phase's tasks must be **executable steps an engineer can follow without
guessing**, sequenced test-first. Write the actual content, not a description:

- Sequence each unit of work as: write the failing test → run it, watch it fail
  → implement the minimal code → run the test, watch it pass → commit.
- Show the actual code or the exact command for any step that changes code or
  runs something — never a paraphrase.
- Reference an exact `path/to/file.ext:line` for every task.

(For numerical/research code, the "failing test" is an assertion against a known
result, analytic case, or invariant — see `ai-research-workflows:hardening-research-code`.)

#### Blocking rule: NO placeholders

A step that says *what* to do without showing *how* is a plan failure. Never
ship any of these — resolve each before saving:

- "TBD", "TODO", "implement later", "fill in details".
- "Add appropriate error handling" / "add validation" / "handle edge cases" —
  name the specific cases and show how each is handled.
- "Write tests for the above" without the actual test code.
- "Similar to Phase N" — repeat the code; phases may be executed out of order.
- Any reference to a type, function, or file not defined in some task.

#### Success Criteria split

**Automated Verification** — commands agents can run without human intervention
(`make test`, `pytest`, file-existence checks, linters).

**Manual Verification** — steps requiring human judgment (UI behavior, UX, edge
cases, performance under real conditions).

#### Blocking rule: NO open questions

If an uncertainty surfaces while writing, **stop**, then either research the
code to resolve it or ask the user. Resume only after it is resolved. A plan
section titled "Open Questions" with unresolved items is **not acceptable**.

### Step 5: Review & Iterate (Collaborative mode)

Present a summary (approach, phase count, criteria counts) and ask for feedback
on phasing, success criteria, technical approach, and edge cases.

For major revisions use the `ai-research-workflows:iterating-plans` skill.

## References and output

- Ground the plan in `docs/rse/specs/research-*.md` (and legacy `.agents/research-*.md`, `.agents/prior-art-*.md`);
  incorporate `docs/rse/specs/experiment-*.md` (or legacy `.agents/experiment-*.md`) when present.
- List all referenced docs in the References section with relative markdown links.
- Create `docs/rse/specs/` if needed; write to `docs/rse/specs/plan-<slug>.md`.
- To revise an existing plan use `ai-research-workflows:iterating-plans`; to execute use
  `ai-research-workflows:implementing-plans`.

## Common Mistakes

- **Confirming approach too late** — writing a detailed multi-phase plan before the user has approved the overall strategy wastes effort; in Collaborative mode, get approach sign-off after Step 2.
- **Placeholder tasks** — "add appropriate error handling", "write tests for the above", or any step that states intent without the concrete code/command is a plan the executor cannot follow; write the actual content.
- **Tests bolted on at the end** — lumping all tests into a trailing Testing Strategy section produces tests-last code; sequence tasks test-first (the failing test before the implementation) within each phase.
- **Vague success criteria** — "works well" or "passes tests" is not a criterion; every item must be a concrete, runnable command or an observable human-verifiable outcome.
- **Unsplit Automated vs Manual** — lumping all criteria together hides what requires human judgment; always separate the two categories explicitly.
- **Leaving open questions in the "final" plan** — a plan section titled "Open Questions" with unresolved items ships ambiguity to the implementer; resolve or explicitly defer every question before saving the plan.
- **Skipping scope boundaries** — omitting the "What We're NOT Doing" section lets scope creep in silently; fill it out even when the answer seems obvious.

## Quality checklist

Before completing the plan verify:

- [ ] All referenced files read completely
- [ ] All research completed and incorporated
- [ ] User consulted on approach and structure (Collaborative mode)
- [ ] Official template used
- [ ] Saved to `docs/rse/specs/plan-<slug>.md`
- [ ] Every phase has `file:line` references
- [ ] Phase tasks are bite-sized and test-first (failing test before implementation)
- [ ] No placeholder tasks ("add appropriate error handling", "write tests for the above", or intent-without-code)
- [ ] Success criteria split into Automated and Manual
- [ ] Criteria are measurable and concrete
- [ ] "What We're NOT Doing" section is filled out
- [ ] NO open questions remain
- [ ] References section links to research and experiment docs


ARGUMENTS: Scope: add an all-sky galaxy-cluster-catalog engine (Planck PSZ2 + MCXC/MCXC-II) to galaxies/v2_0 and replace the fixed 5 Mpc cluster impact cut with an r200-relative criterion. Grounded in docs/rse/specs/research-foreground-galaxies-sightlines.md.

1	"""Additional opt-in catalog engines for galaxy searches."""
2	
3	import numpy as np
4	import pandas as pd
5	import pyvo
6	from astropy import units as u
7	from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

9	from .engines import BaseEngine, VizierEngine

12	NOIRLAB_TAP_URL = "https://datalab.noirlab.edu/tap"
13	
14	
15	def _mask_sentinels(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
16	    """Convert survey sentinel values to NaN without changing nonnumeric fields."""
17	    if df.empty:
18	        return df
19	    return df.replace(-9999, np.nan)
20	
21	
22	def _tap_search_to_dataframe(tap_url: str, adql: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
23	    svc = pyvo.dal.TAPService(tap_url)
24	    table = svc.search(adql).to_table()
25	    return _mask_sentinels(table.to_pandas())
26	
27	
28	def _ensure_standard_columns(df: pd.DataFrame, catalog: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
29	    df = df.copy()
30	    for column in ["ra", "dec", "z"]:
31	        if column not in df.columns:
32	            df[column] = np.nan
33	    df["catalog"] = catalog
34	    return df
35	
36	
37	def _tap_box_query(
38	    tap_url,
39	    table,
40	    coord,
41	    radius,
42	    columns,
43	    where=None,
44	    ra_col="ra",
45	    dec_col="dec",
46	) -> pd.DataFrame:
47	    """Query Data Lab TAP with a box prefilter, then enforce a true sky circle.
48	
49	    NOIRLab Astro Data Lab rejects ADQL geometry functions for these tables
50	    ("function point does not exist"), so use a portable RA/Dec BETWEEN box and
51	    perform the physically correct circular cut with Astropy on the client side.
52	    """
53	    try:
54	        radius_deg = radius.to(u.deg).value
55	        ra0 = coord.ra.deg
56	        dec0 = coord.dec.deg
57	        ddec = radius_deg
58	        cos_dec = max(abs(np.cos(np.radians(dec0))), 1.0e-6)
59	        dra = radius_deg / cos_dec
60	
61	        predicates = [
62	            f"{dec_col} BETWEEN {dec0 - ddec:.12g} AND {dec0 + ddec:.12g}",
63	            f"{ra_col} BETWEEN {ra0 - dra:.12g} AND {ra0 + dra:.12g}",
64	        ]
65	        if where is not None:
66	            predicates.append(f"({where})")
67	
68	        adql = (
69	            f"SELECT {', '.join(columns)} "
70	            f"FROM {table} "
71	            f"WHERE {' AND '.join(predicates)}"
72	        )
73	        df = _tap_search_to_dataframe(tap_url, adql)
74	        if df.empty:
75	            return pd.DataFrame()
76	
77	        matches = SkyCoord(df[ra_col].to_numpy() * u.deg, df[dec_col].to_numpy() * u.deg)
78	        df = df.loc[coord.separation(matches) <= radius].copy()
79	        if df.empty:
80	            return pd.DataFrame()
81	        return df.rename(columns={ra_col: "ra", dec_col: "dec"})
82	    except Exception:
83	        return pd.DataFrame()
84	
85	
86	class DesiDr1Engine(BaseEngine):
87	    def __init__(self, tap_url=NOIRLAB_TAP_URL, require_primary=True):
88	        self.tap_url = tap_url
89	        self.require_primary = require_primary
90	
91	    def query(self, coord, radius) -> pd.DataFrame:
92	        columns = [
93	            "mean_fiber_ra",
94	            "mean_fiber_dec",
95	            "z",
96	            "zerr",
97	            "zwarn",
98	            "spectype",
99	            "deltachi2",
100	            "targetid",
101	            "zcat_primary",
102	        ]
103	        where = "zcat_primary='true' AND zwarn=0" if self.require_primary else "zwarn=0"
104	        df = _tap_box_query(
105	            self.tap_url,
106	            "desi_dr1.zpix",
107	            coord,
108	            radius,
109	            columns,
110	            where=where,
111	            ra_col="mean_fiber_ra",
112	            dec_col="mean_fiber_dec",
113	        )
114	        if df.empty:
115	            return pd.DataFrame()
116	
117	        return _ensure_standard_columns(df, "DESI_DR1")
118	
119	    def query_emfit(self, targetids) -> pd.DataFrame:
120	        return self._query_targetid_table("desi_dr1.emfit", targetids)
121	
122	    def query_agngal(self, targetids) -> pd.DataFrame:
123	        return self._query_targetid_table("desi_dr1.agngal", targetids)
124	
125	    def _query_targetid_table(self, table, targetids) -> pd.DataFrame:
126	        ids = [int(targetid) for targetid in targetids]
127	        if not ids:
128	            return pd.DataFrame()
129	
130	        try:
131	            adql = f"SELECT * FROM {table} WHERE targetid IN ({', '.join(map(str, ids))})"
132	            return _tap_search_to_dataframe(self.tap_url, adql)
133	        except Exception:
134	            return pd.DataFrame()
135	
136	
137	class DesiLsDr10Engine(BaseEngine):
138	    def __init__(self, tap_url=NOIRLAB_TAP_URL):
139	        self.tap_url = tap_url
140	
141	    def query(self, coord, radius) -> pd.DataFrame:
142	        # At Dec +70..+74, Legacy Survey DR10 i-band fields are -9999 sentinels;
143	        # selecting only grz/Wise avoids surfacing nonphysical placeholder data.
144	        columns = [
145	            "ra",
146	            "dec",
147	            "type",
148	            "flux_g",
149	            "flux_r",
150	            "flux_z",
151	            "flux_w1",
152	            "flux_w2",
153	            "flux_w3",
154	            "flux_w4",
155	            "dered_flux_g",
156	            "dered_flux_r",
157	            "dered_flux_z",
158	            "shape_e1",
159	            "shape_e2",
160	            "shape_r",
161	            "sersic",
162	            "ref_cat",
163	        ]
164	        df = _tap_box_query(self.tap_url, "ls_dr10.tractor", coord, radius, columns)
165	        if df.empty:
166	            return pd.DataFrame()
167	
168	        # Data Lab's ls_dr10.photo_z table is empty at these DSA-110 declinations.
169	        df = df.copy()
170	        df["z"] = np.nan
171	        return _ensure_standard_columns(df, "DESI_LS_DR10")
172	
173	
174	class AllWiseEngine(BaseEngine):
175	    def __init__(self):
176	        self._vizier_engine = VizierEngine("II/328/allwise")
177	
178	    def query(self, coord, radius) -> pd.DataFrame:
179	        df = self._vizier_engine.query(coord, radius)
180	        if df.empty:
181	            return pd.DataFrame()
182	
183	        return _ensure_standard_columns(df, "ALLWISE")
184	
185	
186	class GalexAisEngine(BaseEngine):
187	    def __init__(self):
188	        self._vizier_engine = VizierEngine("II/335/galex_ais")
189	
190	    def query(self, coord, radius) -> pd.DataFrame:
191	        df = self._vizier_engine.query(coord, radius)
192	        if df.empty:
193	            return pd.DataFrame()
194	
195	        df = df.copy()
196	        df = df.rename(columns={col: "ebv" for col in ["E(B-V)"] if col in df.columns})
197	        return _ensure_standard_columns(df, "GALEX_AIS")
198	
199	
200	class XscEngine(BaseEngine):
201	    def __init__(self):
202	        self._vizier_engine = VizierEngine("VII/233/xsc")
203	
204	    def query(self, coord, radius) -> pd.DataFrame:
205	        df = self._vizier_engine.query(coord, radius)
206	        if df.empty:

208	
209	        df = df.copy()
210	        df = df.rename(
211	            columns={
212	                col: new_col
213	                for col, new_col in {
214	                    "K.K20e": "Kmag",
215	                    "Kb/a": "axis_ratio",
216	                    "Kpa": "pa_deg",
217	                }.items()
218	                if col in df.columns
219	            }
220	        )
221	        return _ensure_standard_columns(df, "2MASS_XSC")

"""Utility functions for galaxy search v2.0."""

import numpy as np
from astropy import units as u
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
from .config import COSMO

def get_angular_radius(z: float, impact_kpc: float) -> u.Quantity:
    """Calculate angular radius for a given physical impact parameter at redshift z."""
    if z <= 0:
        return 0.0 * u.arcmin
    
    d_a = COSMO.angular_diameter_distance(z)
    with u.set_enabled_equivalencies(u.dimensionless_angles()):
        theta_rad = (impact_kpc * u.kpc / d_a).to(u.rad)
    return theta_rad.to(u.arcmin)

def calculate_impact_parameter(
    ra_gal: float, 
    dec_gal: float, 
    z_gal: float, 
    ra_sight: float, 
    dec_sight: float
) -> float:
    """Calculate physical impact parameter in kpc."""
    if z_gal <= 0:
        return np.nan
        
    c_sight = SkyCoord(ra_sight, dec_sight, unit='deg', frame='icrs')
    c_gal = SkyCoord(ra_gal, dec_gal, unit='deg', frame='icrs')
    sep = c_sight.separation(c_gal)
    
    d_a = COSMO.angular_diameter_distance(z_gal)
    impact_kpc = (sep.radian * d_a).to(u.kpc).value
    return impact_kpc

def parse_coord(ra_str: str, dec_str: str) -> SkyCoord:
    """Parse RA/Dec strings into a SkyCoord object."""
    return SkyCoord(ra_str, dec_str, frame='icrs')

=== halo-mass helpers in generate_galaxy_plots.py ===
54:def estimate_halo_mass(log_mstar):

107:def nfw_enclosed_mass(r, m_halo, r_vir, r_s, c):
def estimate_halo_mass(log_mstar):
    """Halo mass from stellar mass via the Moster+2013 (Eq. 2) SMHM relation.

    M*(M_h) is monotonic in M_h over the plotted range, so we invert Eq. 2
    numerically with brentq rather than using a sinh fitting form. Bracket
    spans dwarf to cluster halos (log M_h in [9.5, 15.5]).
    """
    log_mh = brentq(lambda lmh: _moster_log_mstar(lmh) - log_mstar, 9.5, 15.5)
    return 10 ** log_mh


def get_rvir_and_rs(m_halo_msun, z):
=== get_rvir_and_rs body ===
def get_rvir_and_rs(m_halo_msun, z):
    """R_200 and NFW scale radius r_s in kpc."""
    M_h = m_halo_msun * u.Msun
    H_z = cosmo.H(z)
    G = const.G
    r_vir = ((G * M_h / (100 * H_z**2))**(1/3)).to(u.kpc).value
    # Dutton & Maccio 2014 (MNRAS 441, 3359) c-M relation at z=0.
    log_c = 0.905 - 0.101 * np.log10(m_halo_msun / 1e12)
    # First-order redshift evolution (concentration declines with z); D&M14
    # give d(log c)/dz ~ -0.08 over the low-z regime probed here.
    log_c *= (1.0 - 0.08 * z)
    c = 10**log_c
    r_s = r_vir / c
    return r_vir, r_s, c

=== test_engines.py (pattern: how engines are tested, mocking) ===
import sys
import os
from astropy import units as u
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord

# Add project root to sys.path
sys.path.append(os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '..', '..')))

from galaxies.v2_0.config import TARGETS, VIZIER_CATALOGS
from galaxies.v2_0.utils import parse_coord, get_angular_radius
from galaxies.v2_0.engines import NedEngine, VizierEngine

def test_engines():
    # Test with M31 (Andromeda) to verify engines are working
    coord = SkyCoord(10.6847, 41.2687, unit='deg', frame='icrs')
    radius = 10.0 * u.arcmin
    
    print(f"Testing M31 with {radius} radius")
    
    ned = NedEngine()
    ned_df = ned.query(coord, radius)
    print(f"NED returned {len(ned_df)} rows")
    
    # Try AllWISE as a fallback/test
    wise = VizierEngine("II/328/allwise")
    wise_df = wise.query(coord, radius)
    print(f"AllWISE returned {len(wise_df)} rows")
    if not wise_df.empty:
        print("AllWISE columns:", wise_df.columns.tolist())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_engines()

=== test_search_pipeline.py (how search/_foreground_mask tested) ===
import math

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

from galaxies.v2_0 import search as search_mod
from galaxies.v2_0.config import COSMO
from galaxies.v2_0.engines import _add_desi_stellar_mass
from galaxies.v2_0.search import (
    _deduplicate_matches,
    _enrich_with_ps1_photometry,
    _foreground_mask,
)


def test_foreground_mask_uses_two_sigma_photo_z_error():
    df = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "z": [0.545, 0.610, 0.545],
            "z_phot_err": [0.057, 0.057, math.nan],
            "impact_kpc": [50.0, 50.0, 50.0],
        }
    )

    mask = _foreground_mask(df, z_frb=0.479, z_eps=0.01, impact_kpc=100.0)

    assert mask.tolist() == [True, False, False]


def test_foreground_mask_applies_cluster_impact_threshold():
    # Same impact (1000 kpc): a galaxy is rejected (>100 kpc) but a cluster is
    # kept (<5000 kpc). Cluster flagged via NED 'GClstr' / SDSS 'ClG' classifications.
    df = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "z": [0.10, 0.10, 0.10],
            "impact_kpc": [1000.0, 1000.0, 1000.0],
            "classification": ["G", "GClstr", "ClG"],
        }
    )

    mask = _foreground_mask(
        df, z_frb=0.479, z_eps=0.01, impact_kpc=100.0, cluster_impact_kpc=5000.0
    )

    assert mask.tolist() == [False, True, True]


def test_deduplicate_matches_requires_close_redshift_and_prefers_better_source():
    matches = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "name": ["desi_photo", "ned_spec", "high_z_pair"],
            "ra": [10.0, 10.0 + 1.0 / 3600.0, 10.0 + 2.0 / 3600.0],
            "dec": [20.0, 20.0, 20.0],
            "z": [0.300, 0.301, 0.420],
            "z_phot_err": [0.05, math.nan, 0.05],
            "catalog": ["VII/292/north", "NED", "VII/292/north"],
        }
    )

    deduped = _deduplicate_matches(matches)

    assert deduped["name"].tolist() == ["ned_spec", "high_z_pair"]


def test_deduplicate_matches_prefers_smaller_redshift_error():
    matches = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "name": ["large_error", "small_error"],
            "ra": [10.0, 10.0 + 1.0 / 3600.0],
            "dec": [20.0, 20.0],
            "z": [0.300, 0.301],
            "z_phot_err": [0.05, 0.01],
            "catalog": ["VII/292/north", "VII/292/north"],
        }
    )

    deduped = _deduplicate_matches(matches)

    assert deduped["name"].tolist() == ["small_error"]

    assert deduped["name"].tolist() == ["small_error"]

Context fully read (engine pattern, impact/r200 utils, halo helpers `get_rvir_and_rs`, test conventions). Before writing the plan, four design decisions — all have real physics/scope implications and a recommendation each.

Current-state anchors I'll build on: cluster detection is NED-`Type`-only with a flat 5 Mpc cut (`search.py:31-43,56-74`, `config.py:9-11`); `get_rvir_and_rs(m,z)[0]` already gives R200 from a halo mass (`generate_galaxy_plots.py:65`); the engine pattern is a thin Vizier wrapper (`engines_extra.py:174-183`); cluster mass must be injected as `M_halo` in `build_unified.py:266-277` (today clusters would wrongly get stellar-mass-derived halo mass).

questions.question: What r200-relative impact criterion should replace the flat 5 Mpc cluster cut?

options.label: Keep within 2·r200 (config default)
options.description: Recommended. Research shows meaningful cluster DM needs the sightline within ~1–2 r200; mNFW returns 0 DM beyond r200 anyway, so 2× is safely inclusive. Exposed as a config constant CLUSTER_R200_FACTOR=2.0.

options.label: Keep within 1·r200 (strict)
options.description: Tighter; only sightlines inside the virial sphere. Drops the 1–2 r200 near-misses that still carry tens of pc cm⁻³.

options.label: Keep within 3·r200
options.description: Looser; admits more near-misses with negligible DM. Closer in spirit to the old 5 Mpc but mass-scaled.

questions.question: How should a found cluster's gas be turned into DM/τ?

options.label: Reuse mNFW hot-halo, DM-only
options.description: Recommended (ponytail). Inject catalog M500→M200 as M_halo, run existing dm_halo_mnfw for the ICM column, zero the cool/MgII phase, τ stays hot-only (negligible) — matches Connor 2023 / Lee 2023 (clusters give DM not scattering). Note mNFW-as-ICM as an approximation with a gNFW upgrade path.

options.label: Proper β-model / gNFW ICM
options.description: Implement an Arnaud+2010 gNFW or β-model electron-density profile scaled by catalog R500. More accurate cluster DM but a new physics module — larger diff, more scope.

questions.question: Which all-sky cluster catalogs in this pass?

options.label: PSZ2 + MCXC now, MCXC-II config-ready
options.description: Recommended. Ship Planck PSZ2 (J/A+A/594/A27) + MCXC (J/A+A/534/A109) whose Vizier IDs are known; make the catalog list a config dict so MCXC-II drops in once its Vizier ID is confirmed online (can't verify offline this session). A live smoke query verifies each ID resolves.

options.label: Include MCXC-II now
options.description: Add MCXC-II (arXiv:2402.01538) in this pass too. Risk: its exact Vizier catalog ID can't be verified offline now; a wrong ID would fail the live smoke step and need a follow-up fix.

questions.question: Should the cluster engine run by default?

options.label: On by default
options.description: Recommended. Finding clusters is the explicit goal; wire it into the default engine list. Changes search output (adds cluster rows) — acceptable since the current output finds zero clusters. Still toggleable via ENABLE_CLUSTER_ENGINE.

options.label: Opt-in flag (default off)
options.description: Mirror the existing engines_extra/enricher convention (ENABLE_CLUSTER_ENGINE=False) so default search output stays byte-for-byte unchanged; enable explicitly when running the cluster pass.

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "What r200-relative impact criterion should replace the flat 5 Mpc cluster cut?"="Keep within 2·r200 (config default)", "How should a found cluster's gas be turned into DM/τ?"="Proper β-model / gNFW ICM", "Which all-sky cluster catalogs in this pass?"="Include MCXC-II now", "Should the cluster engine run by default?"="On by default". You can now continue with these answers in mind.
